title: Fail fast on missing feedback item metadata and surface optimizer evaluation errors
description:
As an operator, I want feedback-backed optimizer runs to fail immediately when required item metadata is missing or a score returns ERROR, so that ProcedureTask, Evaluation, and procedure chat clearly explain why no cycles ran.

## Behavior
Feature: Optimizer run failure visibility for broken feedback-backed evaluations

Scenario: Feedback-backed evaluation loses required item metadata
Given an optimizer run starts an initial evaluation from feedback items
And the feedback loading path does not provide the related item metadata payload
When dataset rows are built for evaluation
Then the run fails immediately
And the failure message identifies the feedback item or item id with missing metadata
And TaskStage, Task, Evaluation, and procedure chat show that failure reason

Scenario: Score execution returns ERROR during initial evaluation
Given an optimizer run starts an initial evaluation
When any score execution returns value ERROR
Then the evaluation fails immediately
And the initial evaluation stage is marked failed with the execution error details
And the procedure does not continue to RCA or optimization cycles
And the same failure reason is visible in ProcedureTask, Evaluation, and procedure chat

Scenario: Feedback item loading query fails
Given a date-bounded feedback-backed evaluation
When the GSI query for feedback items fails
Then the run fails instead of falling back to a lower-fidelity metadata-dropping query

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Implemented fail-fast handling for broken feedback-backed optimizer evaluations.

Completed:
- feedback loading now keeps related Item metadata on supported paths and date-bounded GSI failures raise instead of silently falling back
- FeedbackItems dataset construction now fails fast when related Item metadata is missing or unparsable
- Evaluation now treats any score result with value=ERROR as fatal, preserves the underlying score error text, and aborts the run instead of continuing to metrics/RCA
- CLI evaluation failure path now updates Evaluation.errorMessage/status in addition to Task/TaskStage failure state
- Tactus trace sink now records terminal tool responses for started tool calls and emits assistant-visible ALERT_ERROR chat messages for failed tool executions
- targeted tests added/updated for feedback loading, dataset metadata validation, evaluation fail-fast behavior, and trace sink failure visibility

Verification:
- pytest -q plexus/cli/feedback/feedback_service_test.py plexus/data/test_feedback_items.py plexus/test_evaluation_metrics.py plexus/cli/procedure/test_trace_sink.py
- result: 57 passed

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Manual verification complete. Fixed AccuracyEvaluation exception propagation so feedback-backed runs now fail hard instead of drifting into RCA. Verified direct CLI run fails persisted records correctly: evaluation 08a811c0-94f2-49ef-9f6d-91adb0047c3f is FAILED, task 2000e358-6ed2-48d1-bc7c-6a17df5b2c7b is FAILED, Processing stage statusMessage contains the concrete error, and RCA did not start. Root data finding: fetched 307 feedback items through the correct GSI path; 306 item.metadata values are the string '{}' and only 1 includes populated other_data, so the remaining failure is source item metadata for this client rather than Plexus dropping metadata.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Starting a fresh manual optimizer rerun from develop to verify whether the item-metadata backfill resolves the previous fail-fast evaluation error for score 72db3535-2a93-48f3-8900-bb275490cc28 on scorecard 0e48be55-1682-4949-9e7e-3c5b5da1e00a.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Manual optimizer rerun from clean develop failed for a new reason, not the previous metadata fail-fast error. Procedure ba3cbea3-a9ba-433e-ae5c-7a46c1b02a1d / task 0e4b914f-1eac-43e3-a4bd-60b1b3cd31d3 reached Evaluation and found the associated 200-row dataset. Preflight check on the same 30-day feedback window now shows 42/307 items with populated other_data, up from 1/307 previously, so the backfill is helping. The actual failure was plexus_score_pull inside the optimizer: stray stdout polluted the tool result, and the pull itself errored with [Errno 17] File exists: 'scorecards'. Persisted task errorMessage reflects that Lua/plexus_score_pull failure. Separate visibility bug still evident: task ended FAILED but later stages were marked COMPLETED generically instead of stopping at the failing stage.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Implementing the next fix set now: normalize plexus_score_pull to dict-shaped MCP-safe responses, unify local path creation for pulled score files, and correct procedure failure-stage finalization so failed runs do not mark later stages completed.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Implementing the contradictions-report fix set now: shared score-in-scorecard resolution for score IDs, explicit failed ReportBlock/report status instead of fake empty success, and optimizer warnings that distinguish report failure from zero contradictions.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Rerunning procedure 23686f8a-6496-4878-9d39-05f206018a20 directly via CLI with max_iterations=20 for manual verification after contradictions-report fixes.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Stopping active procedure runs and investigating regression causing Agent Misrepresentation evaluations to fail while client-specific missing-metadata failures remain expected.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Removed LangGraph score-runtime checkpointing, added cleanup regression coverage, and manually verified the Agent Misrepresentation feedback evaluation passes the prior failure point (processed_items=61 with no error). Preparing a focused commit and then proceeding to procedure testing.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Removed LangGraph score-runtime checkpointing from score execution. Manual verification: direct Agent Misrepresentation predict still returns a normal result for item 9c929f25-a91f-4db7-8943-5aa93498b8e9--297191731 on version 2ca16cc5-d66d-4065-b875-7d35cedb9be6, and replacement feedback evaluation 98e9535e-70b1-4e08-a282-da9f455bad8d advanced past the old closed-connection failure point (old run failed at processed_items=58; new run observed at processed_items=61 with no error). Proceeding to commit and procedure-level testing.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Consolidating all current worktree changes into one local-only commit on fix/optimizer-fail-fast-metadata-errors per user request. No push yet. Will monitor running procedure a271b380-51d2-4a17-b097-9ca7ea6386b6 / task f94aeb00-3050-4f46-bea9-1caac770158b on a 25-minute heartbeat and intervene if it hits a major failure.
